title: Finance Manager
description:
Our client, a global healthcare solutions provider supporting pharmaceutical products and market access across multiple regions, is seeking a Finance Manager to support its expanding operations in Southeast Asia.

This role will work closely with regional and headquarters stakeholders, managing end-to-end finance operations with exposure to cross-border trade, multi-currency transactions, and group reporting.

Job Responsibilities

  • Oversee full-spectrum financial accounting, including general ledger, accounts payable/receivable, and month-end and year-end closing activities

  • Prepare and review periodic financial statements, management reports, and variance analysis to support business decision-making

  • Manage group reporting requirements, including consolidation of accounts across regional entities and coordination with headquarters

  • Support international business operations, including fund settlements, intercompany transactions, and cross-border payments

  • Monitor and manage foreign exchange (FX) exposure, working with banks and internal stakeholders to mitigate currency risks

  • Handle finance matters related to import and export activities, including trade documentation, cost allocation, customs-related charges, and reconciliation of logistics and landed costs

  • Ensure compliance with Singapore tax regulations, statutory requirements, and internal control policies

  • Liaise with auditors, tax advisors, banks, and external service providers as required

  • Support process improvements, system enhancements, and finance-related projects as the business scales

  • Perform other finance and operational duties as assigned by management

Job Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree or above in Finance, Accounting, or a related discipline

  • 8 years of relevant finance and accounting experience, preferably in a regional or international business environment

  • Prior exposure to group reporting and consolidation, including intercompany accounting, is strongly preferred

  • Experience in industries involving import/export, trading, supply chain, or cross-border operations will be an advantage

  • Strong knowledge of Singapore tax regulations, statutory reporting, and financial compliance, with hands-on accounting experience

  • Solid analytical and problem-solving skills, with strong attention to detail

  • Proficient in accounting systems and financial software; experience with ERP systems is a plus

  • Able to work independently in a fast-growing, dynamic environment with multiple stakeholders

postedCompany.description:
PDR Group
A Global Provider of Integrated Talent Solutions

PDR Group is a global provider of integrated talent solutions, trusted by over 10,000 organizations.
We are committed to shaping the future of talent by connecting individuals from all backgrounds with meaningful opportunities. In doing so, we foster a sustainable talent ecosystem where both talent and businesses can grow and thrive together.

Since 2006, PDR has leveraged nearly two decades of deep industry expertise to build a strong presence across 300+ cities in China and Southeast Asia. Through our “One Contract, Global Delivery” model, we’ve enabled over 6 million individuals to access career opportunities across 20+ industries, including electric vehicles, automotive, consumer electronics, hospitality, manufacturing, healthcare & pharmaceuticals, e-commerce, logistics & supply chain, banking & finance, and contact centers.

Our six core service lines—global executive search, outsourcing, recruitment, staffing, early career programs, and HR consulting—form a scalable, cross-industry platform for delivering tailored, future-ready talent solutions.

We put clients first, combining strategic insight with executional excellence to deliver results at scale. Our industry-leading capabilities and cross-regional network have earned us top recognition and the continued trust of leading organizations, including Fortune 500 companies, across regions.
postedCompany.name: PDR GROUP (SEA) PTE. LTD.
